operating_state: Total revenues increased 2% to $25.5 billion in Q2 2024, driven by energy storage revenue doubling from increased Megapack deployments while automotive sales revenue declined 9% due to lower average selling prices from price reductions and attractive financing options, partially offset by Cybertruck production ramp. Regulatory credits revenue surged 216% to $890 million as other manufacturers scaled back electric vehicle plans.
cost_structure: Total gross margin compressed to 18.0% from 18.2% year-over-year as automotive gross margin declined to 18.5% from 19.2% due to lower average selling prices and temporary manufacturing capacity under-utilization during production ramps, partially offset by lower raw material costs and higher IRA manufacturing credits. Energy storage gross margin expanded significantly to 24.6% from 18.4% due to increased IRA credits and higher-margin business mix.
material_events: Tesla completed global restructuring in Q2 2024 reducing headcount by over 10%, recognizing $583 million in employee termination expenses. Andrew Baglino, Senior Vice President of Powertrain and Energy Engineering, resigned effective April 14, 2024 after 18 years with the company. Stockholders approved redomestication from Delaware to Texas and ratified Elon Musk's 2018 performance-based stock option award at the June 2024 annual meeting.
macro_exposures: Operations face heightened sensitivity to political and regulatory uncertainty affecting trade and environmental policies, compounded by inflationary pressures and central bank interest rate increases impacting vehicle financing affordability. Foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations on significant intercompany balances created unfavorable impacts of $308 million in Q2 without typical hedging protection. Red Sea shipping conflicts and geopolitical tensions disrupted production at Gigafactory Berlin-Brandenburg through supply chain diversions.
forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures to exceed $10.0 billion in 2024 and range between $8.0-$10.0 billion annually in following two fiscal years for global factory expansion, AI infrastructure, and next-generation platform development. Production growth depends on continued factory ramps, autonomy advances, and new product introductions including robotaxi capabilities while expanding Supercharger network capacity to accommodate NACS adoption by other manufacturers.
uncertainty_profile: Manufacturing ramp execution remains subject to new product and technology introduction complexities, concurrent international project management, and potential industry-wide component constraints. Energy storage deployment timing varies significantly quarter-to-quarter based on project milestones and logistics coordination while supplier liquidity challenges in the North American automotive industry could affect component access and pricing.
